programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a mutilated body is found in a car at a scrapyard, the NCIS agents find themselves trying to locate a serial killer. Meanwhile, Abby and McGee head to Mexico and investigate the 20-year-old mystery of a drug dealer's murder. Their actions attract the attention of the head of a local drug cartel, and links begin to emerge between their case, the serial killings and Gibbs's past(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. The victim had been reported missing since she was evacuated before the blast at NCIS headquarters. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan)(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates the murder of a probation officer and is shocked to discover that DNA evidence suggests the killer is connected to him. Yelina takes up the case, and it emerges drugs are at the heart of the crime. Meanwhile, Eric is upset at Calleigh's new relationship with Jake Berkeley. Drama, starring David Caruso and Sofia Milos(n)

Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Kathleen Quinlan(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of a pregnant medical student who has been poisoned - and he soon suspects her boyfriend could be the culprit. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. Meanwhile, Bishop and her husband invite the team out for a special dinner. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A special agent forms a team of geniuses to combat hi-tech threats of the modern age, and hires former child prodigy Walter O'Brien to lead the gang. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion

Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber

Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: An old flame from Northern Ireland turns up on Sergeant McLeod's doorstep with his six-year-old son in tow - a development his wife is not too pleased about. Jeremy has an embarrassing date with Melanie, and soon realises he has more in common with her mother - but she is married to his commanding officer(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road traffic accident turns into a drugs investigation when a quantity of cocaine is found in the crashed vehicle. The driver is dead, but Yvonne is more interested in his passenger, none other than Shirley Moss, who's suffered two broken legs. However, when she begins to question the errant teenager about her continued involvement in drugs, Yvonne soon has reason to believe the youngster isn't the architect of her own downfall - especially after the PC's sister, Beverley, is spotted entering a dealer's flat being staked out by Ken and Suzie. Elsewhere, Mark is upset that his colleagues in SO19 have discovered he is gay, but with the secret now out he throws caution to the wind and decides to take his relationship with Lance one step further - it looks like Sun Hill is about to witness its first gay wedding. With romance in the air, the Christmas party gets into full swing, and new girl Amber certainly enjoys herself - and causes much gossip - by very publicly kissing one of the barm(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark visits Chrissie at work, but the staff shortage means he ends up pitching in to help. She is impressed and urges him to apply for a job, but Tricia is furious at the prospect of him joining the team. Will bravely criticises Connie's lack of management skills and Sean tries to tell Jess how he feels, but she keeps avoiding him(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Kate's anxiety over her financial woes spills over into work, Richard and George clash over treatment of patients, and Jack advises Richard to take it easy because of his multiple sclerosis. A young couple come to blows, driven to their wits' end by their baby's crying(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: It's a difficult day for Yvonne as she faces up to the fact that she was wrong to throw Shirley out of the house. However, the youngster eventually accepts her apology and it looks as if, once she has pulled through this latest mishap, the kindly PC will be able to get her life back on track - until fate deals a terrible blow. Meanwhile, with the tensions in his relationship with Andrea beginning to take their toll, Neil is devastated at the news that she has been critically injured. He turns up at the hospital, only to be faced by Smithy, having already filled his shoes. Lance finally accepts Mark's proposal of marriage after a long day of deliberation, while Gina is surprised to catch a glimpse of Irene Radford. She is convinced her eyes are deceiving her - until Jack confides in her that Sun Hill's crime matriarch is indeed back. Michele Austin, Andrew Lancel, Simon Rouse, Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham and Stefan Booth star(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pete gets caught with his trousers down. Hattie feels that Steve's mind should be turned to more romantic matters(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky makes elaborate plans to celebrate his first anniversary with Sam. Phil gives Grant some advice on how to get back into Sharon's good books(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: When a lorry carrying a load of molten tar crashes, Josie and the team rush to the rescue of a workman trapped under a heavy propane cylinder(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: When faced with a hefty dentist bill, Lovejoy agrees to settle it by investigating the alleged death by drowning of Terence Sullivan, a fellow antiques dealer. It doesn't take long to determine that Sullivan is still alive, having taken up residence with an attractive stewardess. When Charlotte sees Lovejoy's caring side towards the 'widow', she feels she may have misjudged his character and finally gives in to his seductive charm. Pete Postlethwaite guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Susan is intrigued by the interest shown in a seemingly ordinary bungalow she is attempting to sell - but her curiosity leads to her involvement in a murder. John Nettles and Louise Jameson star(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Mrs Boswell is horrified when Billy and Julie have their baby christened in a Protestant church, while Jack and Joey are suspicious about Adrian's new job(n)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace offers the staff accommodation on the premises when their difficult journeys to work lead to demands for a travel allowance, but they soon find flat-sharing is not as easy as it seemed. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean's sister-in-law Penny becomes convinced her husband is having an affair with his dental nurse and insists she move in with Jean and Lionel. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy promises the gang a perfect barbecue in the great outdoors - without a newfangled portable contraption - and takes Clegg, Truly, Alvin and Entwistle into the Dales. He knows the ideal spot - if he can just remember how to find it. Long-running comedy, starring Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Julia refuses to accept the blame when one of her patients dies after surgery, and her investigations reveal an unusual pattern of deaths among alcoholics on the ward(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: British DI Richard Poole is trying to get used to his new Caribbean surroundings when news comes through of another murder - a bride shot through the heart with a spear gun just hours after getting married. Teaming up with Camille, Richard quickly establishes no one entered or left the fifth floor of the hotel where she was celebrating, meaning one of the wedding guests must have been responsible. Mystery drama, starring Ben Miller and Sara Martins, with Frances Barber, Matt Di Angelo and Paterson Joseph(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The wife of a sergeant is murdered while her husband is on duty. A search of the couple's house reveals a list of names, all of whom are rapidly meeting with unfortunate accidents - and it is left to Taggart to discover the connection between the names on the list and the identity of the killer(n)

Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role(n)

programme.desc: Dennis does his bit to cement Anglo-German relations by taking Dagmar for a quiet weekend away. Unfortunately for him, Wayne and Barry blunder in at the crucial moment and reduce his planned seduction to a building-site get-together(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads encounter a fellow Brit while spending a relaxing weekend in the country - but his hunger, exhaustion and evasive behaviour arouse their suspicions. Ray Winstone guests alongside regulars Jimmy Nail, Kevin Whately and Timothy Spall(n)

programme.title: Ms Fisher's Modern Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Part one of two. After two pop singers are electrocuted while performing on a TV music show, Peregrine's impartiality is tested when an old boyfriend is named as the lead suspect in the case. Geraldine Hakewill stars(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A troubled young woman's father is killed three weeks after the death of her mother, leaving the priest to console the bereaved while trying to solve a puzzling murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Sorcha Cusack and Tom Chambers(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is unconvinced when a recently returned PoW is accused of killing his uncle, and it seems that someone will stop at nothing to keep Father Brown from the truth. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd is called upon to investigate when a rugby coach's body is found stripped naked and tied to a goal post. His team's losing streak of 50 straight games has broken national records, and Shepherd is perplexed by a sense of relief in the community. Starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A guest at Higgins and Ruth's wedding makes repeated attempts to kill the groom, but that is just the beginning of the machinations taking place at the Newsome estate(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is called to a murder at a textiles factory, which he is shocked to discover will be his own home in the 21st century. He puts his knowledge of forensics into use to find the killer, but Gene has a simpler approach - the first suspect to speak did it. As battle lines are drawn across CID, it becomes increasingly clear that the Seventies method of detection may in this case be right. John Simm stars(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam discovers many of his colleagues are taking bribes from a local gangster, and resolves to put a stop to police corruption. However, a skeleton in Gene's cupboard makes him reluctant to join the crusade, and his fears are underlined when a young woman mixed up in the case meets a grisly end. The 21st-century detective also can't resist tracking down his mother, who is facing money worries(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Kavanagh defends a left-wing activist accused of murdering someone from the opposite end of the political spectrum - and learns a harsh lesson in the process. To make matters worse, he finds out daughter Kate is having an affair with her married tutor, thus setting the scene for further fireworks. John Thaw stars(n)

programme.desc: Daphne Ledward visits Gawsworth Hall in Cheshire - one of the participants in the National Gardens Scheme - which features an Elizabethan wilderness area(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gabriel learns Smithy reported him over Kerry's rape allegation and the two come to blows - but retribution comes from an unexpected source when the corrupt copper is gunned down by the Sun Hill sniper. Elsewhere, suspicion falls on Shirley after Yvonne's niece accidentally swallows an Ecstasy tablet, Ramani fears Terry is letting his personal preoccupations interfere with his police work, and a shamefaced Jim tells his colleagues that June has left him(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New recruit PC Leela Kapoor proves her mettle on her first day at Sun Hill, but the exposure of a dark secret from her past sours her working relationship with Sheelagh. Jim contemplates stepping outside the law to pay off his gambling debts, while another shooting by the serial sniper leads to an influx of armed officers - one of whom, Sergeant Mark Rollins, just happens to be Lance's boyfriend(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jim sinks to a new low when he pawns valuable police evidence to raise the cash he needs to pay off his gambling debts - then promptly blows the lot on a horse. Andrea refuses to abandon her undercover assignment even though Adam is getting uncomfortably close to the truth. Smithy finds the sniper's rifle and it appears to be only a matter of time before the culprit is finally caught - but not everything is as it seems(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road traffic accident turns into a drugs investigation when a quantity of cocaine is found in the crashed vehicle. The driver is dead, but Yvonne is more interested in his passenger, none other than Shirley Moss, who's suffered two broken legs. programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuthing housewife investigates a spiritualist who appears to be blackmailing her customers, while Geoffrey falls in love, but his secret romance threatens to undermine Hetty's carefully laid trap - and nearly causes him to quit the agency(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A middle-aged widow's family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether he's a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives of Nonnatus House return, with new nurse Barbara, who is anxious to prove herself - only to get off on the wrong foot after a late-night drinks party. A family of severely neglected children is discovered, so Trixie strives to restore their health and bring happiness into their lives, leaving Tom filled with admiration for the woman he loves, while Sister Evangelina finally undergoes tests for her abdominal pains. Shelagh finds she is at the surgery almost as much as she is at home, so the Turners come up with a solution which, for 1960, proves somewhat ground-breaking(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Thomas and Clarissa are just settling down for their respective Christmas Day celebrations when they are called in to investigate a shooting spree at an enormous property owned by Andy McMorris, the CEO of a haulage company. Emilia Fox stars(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Boyd learns he is being removed from the Cold Case unit. For his final job, he re-opens an investigation into the disappearance of 16 homeless teenagers between 1979 and 1982 - the first case he ever worked on. However, as the team struggles to find any new information, Grace worries about her colleague's reasons for revisiting his past - while a guilt-ridden Sarah becomes desperate to make amends for her role in Boyd's sacking. First part of the crime drama's two-part final story, starring Trevor Eve, with Paul McGann(n)

programme.desc: Twm Tisian and Tedi build sandcastles on a visit to the seaside(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Knowing that one objection from a neighbour could thwart his plans to build an extension, Gary goes on a full charm-offensive by hosting a 'civilised' dinner party. Denise, an experienced soiree host, tries to take over the proceedings, while Big Gary is only interested in watching the boxing on Gary's new curve television alongside his gatecrashing golfing mates. Meanwhile, Terri uses the party as an opportunity to showcase her new mobile cocktail business venture. Comedy, with Tom Davis and Laura Checkley(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). Coverage of the opening fixture of this year's tournament from the Principality Stadium, where the hosts start as strong favourites to prevail. This is the first experience of Six Nations rugby as a head coach for Wayne Pivac, who has been chosen as the man to replace Warren Gatland and take Wales forward following an impressive 2019 in which they clinched the Grand Slam and reached the World Cup semi-finals. Italy have finished bottom of the standings in five of the last six years, and also have a new man at the helm, in the shape of interim head coach Franco Smith. Presented by Gabby Logan, with commentary by Andrew Cotter and analysis by Andrea Masi and Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are loved-up couples Mark and Stewart from Leicester and Sindy and Vanel from London, along with uncle-and-niece duo Tony and Kelly from Belfast. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host a dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize. After four weeks of tough auditions, the pressure is on for the four Dance Captains, Todrick Hall, Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse, as they each mentor their three chosen acts through the live Challenge Shows(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie is concerned when he wakes up and realises that Duffy is not in the house and he cannot contact her. Eventually, he is forced to report her as a missing person and when she is found, she is hypothermic. Meanwhile, Lev is in denial about the severity of Luka's tumour, and Ruby decides that Harmony needs her mother(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look ahead to Super Bowl LIV, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida on Sunday night(n)

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with more moral and religious discussions on topical issues, beginning with an edition from James Allen's Girls' School in Dulwich, south London(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant help teams searching for bargains in Kingston upon Thames, and host Charlie Ross finds out about a Victorian photographer who hailed from the town(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores the ruins of Reading Abbey, once one of Europe's finest churches, and the Rev Kate Bottley discovers the deep Christian symbolism of snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale and guests are at the Stade de France to look ahead to this afternoon's match between France and England, and dissect the weekend's other fixtures - which were Ireland v Scotland and Wales v Italy(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper go antique shopping in Wales, where Louise adds to her ever-growing collection of chairs and David takes a risk with items he knows nothing about(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three remaining semi-finalists battle it out for a place in final, and are given the chance to work alongside one of the UK's most exciting chefs, Gareth Ward. First, the chefs are challenged to create a dish of their own design for Gareth to taste, inspired by his own cooking. The competitors will then take on the daunting task of joining Gareth's brigade, taking responsibility for two dishes each during a highly-pressurised service, before returning to the MasterChef kitchen to present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Newcastle couple Josie and Laurence enlist the help of local property expert Dave in Lanzarote to find a perfect rental home for them to spend part of the winter. But will any of the four properties give them everything they want, and will they make the move south for the winter? Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer reaches Sri Lanka, whose strategic location and tropical spices made it a target for invaders and colonisers for centuries. In the north he visits the scenes of vicious battles between the Tamil minority and the Sri Lankan army, traumatic events from which the population is still recovering. On his way to Bangladesh, he hitches a ride on a trawler, highlighting one of the Indian Ocean's fastest-growing industries - providing prawns for the West. But as he reveals, it comes at a price for the environment(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from day 12 of the Grand Slam tournament, which takes place at Melbourne Park, featuring the concluding men's singles semi-final(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer begins the final leg of his journey on the northern tip of Sumatra in western Indonesia, near the epicentre of the 2004 Boxing Day tsunami, finding out how the province of Banda Aceh has undergone many changes since the disaster. He also explores the illegal trade in exotic pets in the Indonesian capital of Jakarta, before heading for Australia, the final country on his epic journey. There he visits the unspoilt wilderness of the Kimberley region, meets a crocodile hunter and goes fishing with natives campaigning to stop the construction of a giant gas plant(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine uncovers a large collection of glassware and stoneware dug up in the north east of England, while David finds an American money box in the shape of a bulldog. Host Paul Martin visits the Oriental Museum at Durham University to find out about the collection of Malcolm McDonald, a British diplomat who was passionate about Asian ceramics(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, mid-way through his rail tour of the country, where he learns how the late-19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. He discovers the fine terminus commissioned by the king and meets a historian and designer, who shares family roots with the present royal dynasty. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following the ordinary men and women who volunteer with the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, risking their lives every day to save others who are in danger(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Helen Flanagan and Jorgie Porter scour some of Scotland's finest antique shops in the hope of finding items that will make the biggest profit at auction in Stirlingshire. Jorgie takes a detour to hit an ace at on a very historic tennis court, while Helen takes a break from the shops to learn about tartan and kilts. Experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton try to keep the duo on the straight and narrow(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams review the extraordinary events that have taken place this week as well as look back over an eventful year in the Cairngorms. With three species of Egrets now breeding in Britain, butterflies on the wing year-round and record-breaking temperatures in the Scottish Highlands, they also explores what winters may be like in the future(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at some 'Queens', inviting Sarah Millican, David Mitchell and Colin Lane to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Road to Brexit
programme.desc: Matt Berry offers his unique take on Brexit in this one-off comedy special to mark the upcoming Article 50 deadline. Reuniting with collaborator Arthur Mathews for the first time since Toast of London, Berry plays rogue historian Michael Squeamish, who's on a mission to discover the origins of Brexit, and offer some interesting opinions on Britain's current plight along the way. Through creative use of archive footage and filmed interviews, the programme unashamedly plays fast and loose with the facts to create a surreal whistle-stop tour of Britain's relationship with Europe(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: In Glasgow, presenter Mary Beard and a panel of guests discuss how culture is responding to the issue of migration. They question how writing and drama can contribute towards an essential conversation about this most topical and controversial of issues, whether migration a valid topic for comedy or satire and how attitudes towards migration reflect a society itself(n)

programme.title: Romeo & Juliet (2013)
programme.desc: The children of two feuding noble houses fall in love, and marry in secret with the aid of a wily priest who hopes to reconcile the two families. However, when the conflict flares up again with fatal consequences, it sets in motion a chain of events that threatens to tear the lovers apart. Adaptation of Shakespeare's tragedy, starring Douglas Booth, Hailee Steinfeld and Paul Giamatti(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of miles of motorways are being made smart by turning the hard shoulder into a live lane, but many experts think they are dangerous for cars that have broken down in them. Richard Bilton speaks to the families of crash victims and asks whether Britain's motorways are becoming death traps(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Trigger-happy space police the Judoon are targeting 21st-century Gloucester, so the Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham race back to Earth in order to prevent them doing too much damage to the cathedral city. But who are the Judoon looking for and what did they do to incur their wrath? Guest starring Neil Stuke, Jo Martin and Ritu Arya(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood share some eerie events from the natural world, including a dust storm which turned an entire city red, and a cloud shaped like a UFO(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall sets out to visit six continents in search of Earth's 60 deadliest animals, with Ethiopia this week's port of call(n)

programme.title: How Earth Made Us
programme.desc: Iain Stewart explores humanity's double-edged relationship with fire, which has simultaneously proven to be a crucial source of energy and a force for destruction. Along the way he takes a therapeutic bath in crude oil, and learns how fire played a vital role in the Industrial Revolution in Britain but held back China's development(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the second of two festive specials. Paralympic gold medallist Kadeena Cox, football star Robbie Savage, history presenter Neil Oliver and Rizzle Kicks singer Jordan Stephens design their dream robot with the help of an experienced engineer, then battle one another in the arena. Who will become the Celebrity Robot Wars champion?(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry gives the contestants just one hour and 45 minutes to create their ultimate curry night, including two different dishes, a traditional bread and a fried snack. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up using an ingredient that features on most people's weekly shopping list, and the cook-off between this week's weakest two contestants. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott selects a range of dishes featuring different types of cheese, including a mozzarella recipe by James Martin, and Mary Berry's blue cheese and goat's cheese tart(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the women's singles final on day 13 of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year. Japan's Naomi Osaka clinched her maiden Australian Open title 12 months ago with a 7-6, 5-7, 6-4 victory over two-time Wimbledon champion Petra Kvitova. It was the third time in four years that the women's final in Melbourne had gone to a nail-biting deciding set(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin explore four glamorous and beautifully designed homes in Florida. Properties include one of the largest homes to feature on the series, a waterfront plot in Miami's Indian Creek comprising two concrete block stacked at right angles and for sale at almost $30million. The duo also visit a self-build inspired by the modernists of the 1940s set in a lush jungle of a garden and a unique home comprising of three separate buildings raised on stilts(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team goes island-hopping around the Western Isles and out to Shetland. Nick Crane hunts for gannets on Eriskay, and Neil Oliver hears the tragic story of a 1918 shipwreck off the Isle of Lewis in which more than 200 servicemen returning from the First World War were drowned. Plus, Miranda Krestovnikoff searches for otters which struggle to survive on the wild coast, and Tessa Dunlop investigates how the epic voyage of HMS Challenger in the 19th century first revealed the secrets of life in the depths of the world's oceans(n)

programme.title: Pipers of the Trenches
programme.desc: Around 2,500 pipers from Scotland, England, Ireland, Canada, Australia and New Zealand served in the First World War and within a year a thousand of them were dead. This film uncovers stories of bravery as descendants of those pipers visit the Somme and Gallipoli, the battlefields where their unarmed relatives accompanied their comrades over the top. Military historians provide insights into the courage of these men and the prospect they faced as they emerged from the trenches and led the troops into battle(n)

programme.title: Whisky Galore! (2016)
programme.desc: In 1943, disaster strikes on the remote Hebridean island of Todday when they run out of whisky. Salvation arrives when a ship carrying 50,000 cases of whisky founders on the island's rocks, but the locals clash with authority in the shape of Home Guard commander Captain Waggett. Comedy, starring James Cosmo, Eddie Izzard and Naomi Battrick(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and the platoon are asked to provide a guard of honour for a royal train carrying George VI that is passing through Walmington-on-Sea, but another locomotive confounds their plans. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues to explore Alaska, from the remote former goldrush settlement of Talkeetna to the railwayless state capital, Juneau. Along the way, he helps a homesteader fell a tree to clear land for a log cabin, discovers how 100 years ago photographers captured the beauty of the Alaskan landscape and meets scientists studying the Northern Lights(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig calls for a bit of 'Quiet' in this extended version of the panel show, this time inviting Andrew Maxwell, Sara Pascoe and Jimmy Carr to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ellie Taylor from BBC Two's The Mash Report presents stand-up comedy from London's Hammersmith Apollo, inviting rising star Fin Taylor and the provocative Tez Ilyas to the stage(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: After the betrayals that saw him on a murder charge and on the ropes, Franklin has squared things with his family and his gang rule the streets. But the streets are changing. Having got Franklin out of jail, Teddy is still running the entire secret drugs operation to fund the Contras but he is vulnerable and exposed. He needs new supply routes and, to his shock, a new pilot. Hard-hitting drama about the beginnings of the crack epidemic in 1980s Los Angeles, starring Damson Idris and Carter Hudson(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Since Manboy's gang made a move, Leon does not trust their old schoolfriend - but Franklin is playing a longer game. Officer Wright makes his first move on Franklin's operation(n)

programme.title: Up in the Air (2009)
programme.desc: A corporate downsizing expert loves his life, detached from everyday existence and striving to achieve a personal goal of 10 million accumulated air miles. However, his lifestyle comes under threat by an up-and-coming executive with a revolutionary approach that would make his role all but obsolete. Comedy drama, starring George Clooney, Vera Farmiga, Anna Kendrick, Jason Bateman and Amy Morton(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke have one day to tame a wild, riverside patch belonging to a houseboat in Newhaven, Sussex(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler tries to become self-sufficient in fruit and vegetables as she uses her small back garden to plant, grow and harvest her own produce. She begins with peas and beans, and provides useful growing tips as well as recipes including broad-bean falafels and pea-shoot cocktails. She also forages for willow to make plant supports and buys two chickens(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Helen Flanagan and Jorgie Porter scour some of Scotland's finest antique shops in the hope of finding items that will make the biggest profit at auction in Stirlingshire. Jorgie takes a detour to hit an ace at on a very historic tennis court, while Helen takes a break from the shops to learn about tartan and kilts. Experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton try to keep the duo on the straight and narrow(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Reports on rural developments from the Peak District. Anita Rani finds out about a campaign to save Bamford's village pub and joins a group of ramblers on a trip into the surrounding countryside. Sean Fletcher meets a grocer who pulls together all the best produce from local farmers, while Helen Skelton goes rock climbing. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes some new arrivals on the farm and Tom Heap reports on the financial impact of environmentally friendly farming(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Mary visits Goodwood House, home to the March family, famous for their iconic horse racing and motoring events. She is taken on a thrilling lap of Goodwood motor circuit by Lord March, before attending one of his infamous dinner parties where 300 guests dine in style in splendid state rooms. Mary also learns more about the Estate's organic farm, before preparing a Coq-au-Vin and a four-tier cake for a cricket tea set in one of the oldest cricket clubs in the world(n)

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: The Hairy Biker fulfils a boyhood dream by visiting the pyramids at Giza, goes to the oasis of Al Fayoum in search of a dish with mythical properties and heads to a place on the banks of the Nile where ancient man first turned to farming. To mark the end of his journey, Dave is invited by a family of Nubians for a feast to celebrate the end of Eid Al Adha, and helps prepare a traditional meal that has become famous across the entire Arab world(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents action from the men's singles final on day 14 of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year. Novak Djokovic defeated Rafael Nadal in straight sets last year to win his seventh title here(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites veteran broadcaster and entertainer Nicholas Parsons to delve into his youth in Hampstead Heath. The chef cooks nostalgic dishes in an outdoor kitchen, hoping to transport Nicholas back to his mother's cooking and reflect the kind of food he likes eating now(n)

programme.title: The Train (1964)
programme.desc: As the Allies look set to liberate Paris from the Nazi forces, the Germans resolve to take all they can from the city - and plot to transport national treasures back to Berlin. However, bold French Resistance forces vow to stop them, enlisting the help of a train inspector along the way. Second World War adventure, starring Burt Lancaster, Paul Scofield, Jeanne Moreau and Michel Simon(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist explores some of the world's most dangerous environments, beginning with a journey to the Arctic. The objective is to discover how rising temperatures are affecting the environment, wildlife and the local people. Steve begins his expedition on the east coast of Greenland, where he assembles a team of experts to join him. The first stage of the journey involves kayaking across the world's largest fjord, with the explorers braving lethal and ever shifting sea ice, vast icebergs and hungry polar bears(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the World Cup meeting in Schladming, Austria, featuring the iconic night slalom. Plus, highlights of the world's longest boardercross downhill race, the Inferno from Feldberg, Germany(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The couples are split up for a navigation challenge that pits men against women on a trek through the wilderness. A local search and rescue expert oversees their efforts and offers his thoughts on which couple should be the next to be eliminated from the contest. After another pair has gone home, the remaining competitors are taken to a gun range to be introduced to the Alaskan attitude to firearms and put their marksmanship to the test(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Burnley v Arsenal at Turf Moor. Spurs and the Citizens shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw in August's reverse fixture, while the Gunners recorded a 2-1 home win over the Clarets. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: A preview of tonight's showpiece match between Kansas City Chiefs and San Francisco 49ers at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: As the United Kingdom prepares to leave the European Union, Katty Kay, Christian Fraser and Ros Atkins lead a special live programme from London and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on February 16, featuring performances by Def Leppard, Simple Minds, Gloria Estefan, Poison, Edie Brickell and the New Bohemians, Pop Will Eat Itself, Debbie Gibson, Texas, Sam Brown, the Style Council, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and S-Express(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The writer and composer looks at the rise of the all-conquering contemporary blockbuster, from Evita to Les Miserables and The Lion King. He also sees how these mega-musicals have met with unexpected competition from quirky hits such as The Rocky Horror Show and Avenue Q. Contributors include Tim Rice, Alan Menkin and Robert Lopez(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Susie Mathis present the edition first shown on February 23, featuring performances by S-Express, Michael Jackson, Edie Brickell and the New Bohemians, Rick Astley, Tyree featuring Kool Rock Stead, Living in a Box, Tone Loc, Dusty Springfield, Hue and Cry, Michael Ball, Simple Minds, and Bananarama and Lananeeneenoonoo(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The performer explores the transcendental powers of music and how certain sounds have the ability to move transport the mind and even help people briefly 'escape the world'. Stuart travels to Morocco to discover the polyrhythms of Gnawa and back to Wells Cathedral to understand the mechanics of choral polyphony. He meets Kanye West collaborator Caroline Shaw to examine melody and the effect of the human voice, CeCe Winans to discuss the roots of Gospel, takes a gong bath in New York and visits minimalist master Steve Reich to unpack the trance inducing powers of repetition(n)

programme.title: Buddy Holly: Rave On
programme.desc: Rock 'n' roll started with Elvis, but some believe that pop music started with Buddy Holly and the Crickets. As a songwriter, Holly revolutionised the genre by introducing dynamic new rhythms and unpredictable melodies beyond its traditional blues roots. In his songs written and recorded in the late '50s, it is already possible to hear the beat group sound of the 1960s, and beyond. This documentary tells the story of Holly's tragically short life and career through interviews with those who knew him and worked with him. Contributors include Don McLean, Paul Anka, Don Everly, Hank Marvin and Bob Harris(n)

programme.title: Reginald D Hunter's Songs of the South
programme.desc: The London-based comedian continues his exploration of the American South through its songs both past and present in Alabama, where he attends a Lynyrd Skynyrd gig and learns about the city of Muscle Shoals' role in the development of soul. Reg then heads to his home state of Georgia, where he visits Athens - where rock bands the B52s and REM originated - and Atlanta, the birthplace of civil rights leader Martin Luther King and rapper Ludacris. Featuring contributions by Arrested Development, St Paul and the Broken Bones, Clarence Carter and Sharon Jones(n)

programme.title: The Story of Pulp's Common People
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins of the indie band's song about class differences in the 1990s. Frontman Jarvis Cocker returns to St Martin's College, where he met the girl who became the inspiration for the track's heroine, while the band recall the single's success. With contributions from Sadie Frost, Vic Reeves, Franz Ferdinand and Kaiser Chiefs(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The performer explores the transcendental powers of music and how certain sounds have the ability to move transport the mind and even help people briefly 'escape the world'. Stuart travels to Morocco to discover the polyrhythms of Gnawa and back to Wells Cathedral to understand the mechanics of choral polyphony. programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand

He also sees how these mega-musicals have met with unexpected competition from quirky hits such as The Rocky Horror Show and Avenue Q. Contributors include Tim Rice, Alan Menkin and Robert Lopez(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce(n)

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim is horrified at allegations of phone-tapping and emphatically denies they are true - until Sir Humphrey informs him otherwise. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Dangerous Earth
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski examines the latest scientific insight into auroras - dancing lights in the night sky that have fascinated cultures throughout history(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: While dealing with the arrival of boatloads of migrants from across the Mediterranean - and with the harrowing human tragedy that accompanies them - the detective stumbles upon the murder of a dressmaker who had set up shop in Vigata. Montalbano must uncover the secrets behind the victim's life to get at the truth behind her death. Italian detective drama based on novels by Andrea Camilleri, starring Luca Zingaretti(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Berlin
programme.desc: Clive takes a light-hearted look at the German capital, revealing the huge changes that have taken place there since reunification - architecturally, socially and culturally. He mingles with the weird and wonderful youth of the city, keeps a last appointment at the notorious border-crossing Checkpoint Charlie, and eventually finds himself cold-shouldered off the road by former East Berliners in convertibles(n)

programme.title: Frantz (2016)
programme.desc: A German woman mourning her fiance's death in the First World War is slowly drawn to the French soldier who also visits his grave. Romantic drama, starring Pierre Niney, Paula Beer and Ernst Stotzner. In French and German(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on February 16, featuring performances by Def Leppard, Simple Minds, Gloria Estefan, Poison, Edie Brickell and the New Bohemians, Pop Will Eat Itself, Debbie Gibson, Texas, Sam Brown, the Style Council, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and S-Express(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester City v Arsenal at the Academy Stadium and Chelsea v West Ham United at Kingsmeadow, as City, Arsenal and Chelsea continued their three-way battle for the title(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia through the seasons, beginning during spring in Nant Ffrancon in the Ogwen Valley. A mountain guide takes him high up above the glacial landscape of Cwm Idwal, before the wildlife expert heads to a hidden river gorge in full flow, listens to the dawn chorus in an ancient woodland, and visits Bird Rock, an imposing crag in the Dysynni Valley(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: From enormous tortoises and deep-diving lizards to fish-eating snakes and birds that hunt giant, venomous centipedes, the wildlife of the Galapagos Islands is unique and bizarre. However, the wilderness that inspired Charles Darwin and the theory of evolution is becoming ever-more crowded due to increased migration and tourism. David Attenborough narrates this documentary, which examines how the Pacific archipelago's animals are adapting to their changing circumstances(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Documentary using clips from the BBC archives to explore how six decades of TV has influenced the understanding of the arts. In the first edition, Kate Bryan looks at the use of the nude by both artists and film-makers as a means to shock their audiences and challenge conventions(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Investigation into attitudes toward nudity, suggesting people's embarrassment about their bodies can be traced back to a specific stage of evolution - the period when mankind's ancestors lost their fur. Scientists from Africa to America add an informed perspective, and volunteers take a series of tests designed to assess reactions to the naked human form, shedding new light on humanity's physical and social development(n)

programme.title: Fig Leaf: The Biggest Cover Up in History
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the history of the fig leaf's use throughout 2,000 years of Western art and ethics. He explains how the work of Michelangelo fuelled the Fig Leaf Campaign censorship effort, how Bernini created a new form of erotica with his use of the slipping gauze, and how Rodin brought depictions of nudity back to the public's attention. The presenter argues that Victorians had a more sophisticated and mature attitude to sexuality than is found today, and offers his view that modern art could be redeemed from cheap sensation by the return of the fig leaf(n)
