programme.desc: A fable about why baboons have distinctive bottoms. Based on a collection of African folk tales(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When the school play is suddenly postponed, Sam is unimpressed but Kobi convinces the children to donate some time to helping the local community centre instead by providing food, shelter and good cheer.(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: A new teacher starts at Struay Primary School, and brings with him his son John. At first Katie does not like the new boy, but she soon starts to enjoy his stories about the places where he used to live, and starts to think about the island in a new light(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah & Duck join Scooter Boy at the cinema to watch his favourite film, but the sound doesn't seem to be working(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The gang members have been growing a pumpkin all through summer for the Halloween party at school, and everyone has decided what fancy dress to wear - except for Lola(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo needs to go to the shops, but Tee is more interested in building a rocket out of cardboard boxes(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny is stuck for ideas for a new tune, so asks Mother and Major what kind of tune they would most like to hear. Major wants something bold and stirring, while Mother would like something light and charming. Tiny's best attempts to please them both come to nothing, until a ride on a Skymoo gives her the inspiration she needs. Narrated by Michael Palin(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka cannot find any stones in the garden, so his friends help him. Narrated by Derek Jacobi(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Mr Tumble reads and signs tonight's calming Bedtime Story all about getting ready for bed(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar bring their evidence to court, as they present their arguments to a select jury of 12 people who have featured across the week. Eventually, the jury leaves to consult in a separate room. There are strong arguments on all sides before the jury members cast their votes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Daniel clash, Bear's inconvenient secret is finally out, while social influencer Luton Bradley's lifestyle proves not to be so glamorous as it seems(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by ex-Wales player James Hook, comedian Hugh Dennis and former Wales captain Rachel Taylor, as they take an entertaining look at Wales' prospects at this year's Six Nations(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering how to have a good time. It's a story of horn blowing, bog snorkelling and Elvis impersonators, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Billy naively thinks he and Bobby can make some easy money from a 'simple' house clearance. Nats is rescued by Bobby - or so she thinks. Comedy about a family of chancers, low on cash but high on spirit - determined to survive through any means necessary. Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Alexandria Riley star(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, appearing as Doctor Robotnik in the big-screen adaptation of classic video game Sonic the Hedgehog, and Australian actress Margot Robbie, reprising her role as Harley Quinn in superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Knowing that one objection from a neighbour could thwart his plans to build an extension, Gary goes on a full charm-offensive by hosting a 'civilised' dinner party. Denise, an experienced soiree host, tries to take over the proceedings, while Big Gary is only interested in watching the boxing on Gary's new curve television alongside his gatecrashing golfing mates. Meanwhile, Terri uses the party as an opportunity to showcase her new mobile cocktail business venture. Comedy, with Tom Davis and Laura Checkley(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' take on the iconic Snatch Game challenge, where they must deliver their best celebrity impersonations against a classic TV game show backdrop. Spice Girl legend Geri Horner joins singer Michelle Visage and comedian Alan Carr on the judging panel, while special guests Lorraine Kelly and Stacey Dooley also join in the fun(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Danilo Cortellini, and Sam Evans and Shauna Guinn, and special guest Clara Amfo faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by ex-Wales player James Hook, comedian Hugh Dennis and former Wales captain Rachel Taylor, as they take an entertaining look at Wales' prospects at this year's Six Nations(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v Southampton and Leicester City v Chelsea in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: The presenter looks ahead to the championship, speaking to the likes of new Wales head coach Wayne Pivac, his star outside-half Dan Biggar and former Ireland captain Jamie Heaslip about preparations for the tournament. Eddie also looks back at some of the most memorable days in the competition's history(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). Coverage of the opening fixture of this year's tournament from the Principality Stadium, where the hosts start as strong favourites to prevail. This is the first experience of Six Nations rugby as a head coach for Wayne Pivac, who has been chosen as the man to replace Warren Gatland and take Wales forward following an impressive 2019 in which they clinched the Grand Slam and reached the World Cup semi-finals. Italy have finished bottom of the standings in five of the last six years, and also have a new man at the helm, in the shape of interim head coach Franco Smith. Presented by Gabby Logan, with commentary by Andrew Cotter and analysis by Andrea Masi and Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are loved-up couples Mark and Stewart from Leicester and Sindy and Vanel from London, along with uncle-and-niece duo Tony and Kelly from Belfast. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host a dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize. After four weeks of tough auditions, the pressure is on for the four Dance Captains, Todrick Hall, Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse, as they each mentor their three chosen acts through the live Challenge Shows(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie is concerned when he wakes up and realises that Duffy is not in the house and he cannot contact her. Eventually, he is forced to report her as a missing person and when she is found, she is hypothermic. Meanwhile, Lev is in denial about the severity of Luka's tumour, and Ruby decides that Harmony needs her mother(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look ahead to Super Bowl LIV, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida on Sunday night(n)

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by Cemlyn Davies(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with more moral and religious discussions on topical issues, beginning with an edition from James Allen's Girls' School in Dulwich, south London(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, her discovering how to have a good time. It's a story of horn blowing, bog snorkelling and Elvis impersonators, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores the ruins of Reading Abbey, once one of Europe's finest churches, and the Rev Kate Bottley discovers the deep Christian symbolism of snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale and guests are at the Stade de France to look ahead to this afternoon's match between France and England, and dissect the weekend's other fixtures - which were Ireland v Scotland and Wales v Italy(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

This is the first experience of Six Nations rugby as a head coach for Wayne Pivac, who has been chosen as the man to replace Warren Gatland and take Wales forward following an impressive 2019 in which they clinched the Grand Slam and reached the World Cup semi-finals. Italy have finished bottom of the standings in five of the last six years, and also have a new man at the helm, in the shape of interim head coach Franco Smith. Presented by Gabby Logan, with commentary by Andrew Cotter and analysis by Andrea Masi and Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are loved-up couples Mark and Stewart from Leicester and Sindy and Vanel from London, along with uncle-and-niece duo Tony and Kelly from Belfast. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host a dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize. After four weeks of tough auditions, the pressure is on for the four Dance Captains, Todrick Hall, Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse, as they each mentor their three chosen acts through the live Challenge Shows(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie is concerned when he wakes up and realises that Duffy is not in the house and he cannot contact her. Eventually, he is forced to report her as a missing person and when she is found, she is hypothermic. Meanwhile, Lev is in denial about the severity of Luka's tumour, and Ruby decides that Harmony needs her mother(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look ahead to Super Bowl LIV, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida on Sunday night(n)

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest fixtures in the Premier League, including Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield and Leicester City v Chelsea at the King Power Stadium. The Reds had won their previous five meetings with the Saints, including a 2-1 victory in the reverse fixture at St Mary's Stadium in August. Former Southampton player Sadio Mane opened the scoring, with strike partner Roberto Firmino adding a second before Danny Ings grabbed a late consolation for the home side against his former employers, following a goalkeeping error by Adrian. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Aston Villa at the Vitality Stadium, Crystal Palace v Sheffield United at Selhurst Park, Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford, Newcastle United v Norwich City at St James' Park, Watford v Everton at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: Gordon Brewer and guests look at the week's events at Westminster and Holyrood(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell returns with more moral and religious discussions on topical issues, beginning with an edition from James Allen's Girls' School in Dulwich, south London(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant help teams searching for bargains in Kingston upon Thames, and host Charlie Ross finds out about a Victorian photographer who hailed from the town(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores the ruins of Reading Abbey, once one of Europe's finest churches, and the Rev Kate Bottley discovers the deep Christian symbolism of snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale and guests are at the Stade de France to look ahead to this afternoon's match between France and England, and dissect the weekend's other fixtures - which were Ireland v Scotland and Wales v Italy(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the third and final fixture of the opening weekend of the campaign, as the fierce rivals clash at the Stade de France. England fell at the final hurdle in last year's World Cup, but can take heart from making great strides since last year's Six Nations and from a strength in depth that the others will find hard to match. France have a new coach in former scrum-half Fabien Galthie, who will be hoping his new charges can continue their excellent recent record against England in Paris, where they've sent the visitors back home defeated on three of the last four occasions. John Inverdale hosts, with expert analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott and Thomas Castaignede, while Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Paul O'Connell provide the commentary(n)

programme.title: Sir David Jason at 80: A Lovely Jubbly Celebration
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas takes a trip through the BBC archives to mark the 80th birthday of actor David Jason, one of Britain's biggest and best-loved stars of the small screen. With interviews, insights and Only Fools and Horses rarities, Johnny looks at the man behind Granville and Del Boy and pieces together the story of David's rise to the top of the comedy tree.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars(n)

programme.title: British Academy Film Awards
programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts the ceremony live from the Royal Albert Hall in London, with 1917, The Irishman, Joker, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Parasite up for Best Film. Also in the running for awards are Leonardo DiCaprio, Charlize Theron, Joaquin Phoenix, Quentin Tarantino, Saoirse Ronan, Adam Driver, Jonathan Pryce, Bong Joon Ho, Brad Pitt, Al Pacino, Joe Pesci, Taika Waititi, Margot Robbie, Greta Gerwig, Laura Dern, Florence Pugh, Renee Zellweger and Scarlett Johansson(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Kansas City Chiefs v San Francisco 49ers (Kick-off 11.30pm). Coverage of the NFL's showpiece match, which takes place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, Florida, as the AFC and NFC champions face each other for the coveted Vince Lombardi trophy. The Chiefs - led by last year's NFL MVP Patrick Mahomes - are appearing in their first Super Bowl since 1970, when they defeated Minnesota Vikings 23-7. The 49ers have won five Super Bowl championships, but their most recent triumph came on this same ground 25 years ago, when they beat San Diego Chargers 49-26 thanks largely to quarterback Steve Young's six touchdown passes. Last year, the title was won by Tom Brady's New England Patriots, who triumphed 13-3 over the Los Angeles Rams in Atlanta, Georgia - the lowest-scoring Super Bowl game in history. The Patriots were eliminated by the Tennessee Titans in the Wild Card round this season, denying Brady and head coach Bill Belichick yet another NFL Championship(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Chris Daw and Ayesha Nayyar bring their evidence to court, as they present their arguments to a select jury of 12 people who have featured across the week. Eventually, the jury leaves to consult in a separate room. There are strong arguments on all sides before the jury members cast their votes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An adventure centre and a garage are burgled, but the businesses' own cameras do not show the thief's face, with the victims turning to street cameras for help. Plus, a partially blind man is deceived at a cashpoint by an unscrupulous thief. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Daniel clash, Bear's inconvenient secret is finally out, while social influencer Luton Bradley's lifestyle proves not to be so glamorous as it seems(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper charge on the wily, windy moors of West Yorkshire, questing for antiques for their final auction. Louise gets her hands on a very early labour-saving device, as well as an illuminating bit of retro chic. David takes a shine to some theatre seats, but things get complicated when he tries to work out how many he can afford(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon takes Kathy's keys and sneaks into Phil's house - only to be caught red-handed - and Mick worries when Linda is nowhere to be found, unaware she has taken Ollie out of school for the day. Suki settles into Walford, but it soon becomes clear she has an ulterior motive and will go to any lengths to reunite her family. Keegan has a new lease of life and throws himself into work, while Whitney makes a horrifying discovery(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society stages a family saga of lust and betrayal set in the American Deep South. A designer error means that one of the sets has been built at a literal 90 degrees to the vertical. Too expensive to re-stage, the gang decides to turn the cameras on their side and pretend that everything's normal. Gravity, however, has other ideas, and even getting in and out of chairs proves potentially limb-threatening. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McClean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Knowing that one objection from a neighbour could thwart his plans to build an extension, Gary goes on a full charm-offensive by hosting a 'civilised' dinner party. Denise, an experienced soiree host, tries to take over the proceedings, while Big Gary is only interested in watching the boxing on Gary's new curve television alongside his gatecrashing golfing mates. Meanwhile, Terri uses the party as an opportunity to showcase her new mobile cocktail business venture. Comedy, with Tom Davis and Laura Checkley(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: BBC News Special
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces coverage of the moment the United Kingdom leaves the European Union. The departure was made possible after Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Brexit deal was backed by MPs in the wake of the general election. From February, the country enters an 11-month transition period to prepare for its new relationship with the EU(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood great Jim Carrey, appearing as Doctor Robotnik in the big-screen adaptation of classic video game Sonic the Hedgehog, and Australian actress Margot Robbie, reprising her role as Harley Quinn in superhero film Birds of Prey. British star Daniel Kaluuya and newcomer Jodie Turner-Smith are also on the guest list, discussing romantic drama Queen & Slim. Plus, Lewis Capaldi performs his current single, Before You Go(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The queens' take on the iconic Snatch Game challenge, where they must deliver their best celebrity impersonations against a classic TV game show backdrop. Spice Girl legend Geri Horner joins singer Michelle Visage and comedian Alan Carr on the judging panel, while special guests Lorraine Kelly and Stacey Dooley also join in the fun(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Danilo Cortellini, and Sam Evans and Shauna Guinn, and special guest Clara Amfo faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Bradley Stoke, Bristol, to come to the horticultural aid of Laura and Andrew, who require a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v Southampton and Leicester City v Chelsea in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: The presenter looks ahead to the championship, speaking to the likes of new Wales head coach Wayne Pivac, his star outside-half Dan Biggar and former Ireland captain Jamie Heaslip about preparations for the tournament. Eddie also looks back at some of the most memorable days in the competition's history(n)

They're joined in the studio by actress and Casualty star Cathy Shipton, to talk about her emotional final episode on the show and how it feels to be leaving after all this time(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five friends, calling themselves the Leading Ladies, answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Alex, Mark, Bev and Diana work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Amy is concerned by what she sees, and is unimpressed with Victoria, while someone is in court(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria is shocked to hear that Daniel failed to have Bertie vaccinated, while Sally returns and is horrified to realise everyone knows Tim's a bigamist. As Alya assures Yasmeen that her drinking is fine, Geoff does his best to undermine her confidence(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After waking from a nap, Daniel confuses Bethany for Sinead, and kisses her. Geoff continues to spread lies about Yasmeen's drinking, while Steve and Tracy are railroaded into attending Ken's book club as part of their relationship-building exercise(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: Tom Bradby is joined by special guests in the studio, and ITV News presenters and correspondents around the country, for the countdown to the historic moment the UK leaves the European Union. Tonight's event comes after three extensions and three-and-a-half years following the referendum on the issue of continued membership. Plus, the day's other national and international headlines.(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam reaches out to Laura, but risks upsetting his family as a result, while Karen and Jenny's weekend away is hijacked by their mothers. Pete's attempts to reunite the Three Amigos ends in disaster. John Thomson, James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst star in Mike Bullen's long-running comedy drama, with Sacha Parkinson, Gemma Jones, Clare Keelan and Sunetra Sarker(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Today's mystery host is football legend Peter Shilton. Will the diners tackle his cunning clues and walk off with the cash or can the famous goalie save himself from the dreaded dishes? Presented by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technologies and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Twinkle says goodbye to her friends to rejoin her people. But when the team - now the Super 3 - have to stave off a Sykronian invasion, they cannot do it without Twinkle(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur(n)

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Fast flowing waters await Danny in today's dare as he takes on a white-water rafting course including the UK's steepest man-made drop.(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom is framed for a crime he did not commit and is sent to prison. Jerry works to clear his name(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other(n)

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean digs up Bruiser Snr's medallion and rushes off to the local museum convinced he's found treasure.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave's first plane journey doesn't go to plan when the air pressure and the need to go to the toilet makes his head swell.(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: When a rare spider bites a scientist, Scott must locate the anti-venom carrier drone in a dense rainforest. Guest starring Peter Davison(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the studio by Soft Cell singer Marc Almond, antiques expert Philip Serrell, and chefs Gareth Ward and Tessa Bramley. Oz Clarke presents a guide to the best Argentinean wines to be found in supermarkets(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth serves up his father's paella, he and Ian Theasby bake a Victoria sponge and they are joined by Day Radley, founder of the Vegan Chef School, who demonstrates her beetroot ravioli. Henry and Ian also show how to jazz up home-made houmous and make a Texan-style pizza, with spiced tofu and bourbon sauce(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Ranj Singh present the consumer health show. Volunteers help put two more diets through their paces - the Eat What You Like & Lose Weight for Life cook book and Noom, an app that has been trending worldwide. How will they rank on the leaderboard? Also, Sian test a new super grain, pea milk and a vegetable sheeter, and Ranj investigates technology and gadgets that are designed to boost willpower when it comes to dieting(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform yet again, hoping to get a high score from the judges - not to mention plenty of viewer votes - and avoid following Trisha Goddard and Lucrezia Millarini in being eliminated from the contest. And while the votes are being counted, Jane Torvill and Christopher Dean take to the ice to show the contestants how it should be done. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 4.45pm). Coverage from the Aviva Stadium in Dublin, where Andy Farrell takes charge of his first Six Nations match as head coach of Ireland. The sides will be perfectly familiar with each other, having met in the pool stage of the World Cup in Japan in September, when Ireland came out on top 27-3 in a relatively one-sided encounter. However, both teams were to come a cropper at the hands of the host nation, meaning elimination for Scotland and a quarter-final against New Zealand for the Irish, in which they were comprehensively outplayed. The Scots still have Gregor Townsend at the helm, with the pressure on the former fly-half to harness the undoubted talent that his squad possesses into positive results. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Brian O'Driscoll and Ian McGeechan, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Scott Hastings and Gordon D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a special double elimination show, in which two more singers will be unmasked. Sharon and Kelly Osbourne are today's special guests on the panel, joining series regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall as they attempt to guess the identities of the six famous faces performing from behind bizarre costumes(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: The blind auditions enter their fifth week, as coaches will.i.am, Sir Tom Jones, Meghan Trainor and Olly Murs continue their search for the next singing superstar. The acts will be hoping to secure a place on a coach's team and take a step closer to winning a life-changing record deal(n)

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith takes 1980s pop icon Matt Goss from Bros shopping around Mayfair, and 2018 I'm a Celebrity runner-up Emily Atack in search of a new bed(n)

programme.title: American Sniper (2014)
programme.desc: A Navy Seal becomes a legend to his colleagues thanks to his skills as a deadly sniper. On his return to the US following four tours of duty in Iraq, he finds living an ordinary life as a husband and father far more difficult to deal with than the demands of warfare. Biopic based on the experiences of real-life Iraq conflict veteran Chris Kyle, directed by Clint Eastwood and starring Bradley Cooper, Sienna Miller and Kyle Gallner(n)

programme.title: Rebound
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the quickfire quiz that tests the general knowledge of six contestants, who go head to head in a bid to walk away with a big cash prize. The players must give as many correct answers as they can in the time it takes the 'Rebound Bar' to move across the studio, with the money on offer rising accordingly(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Rock Brock becomes the target of an angry elf after he breaks his magic cauldron during a battle with a ferocious dragon - or so he says(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: When a popular vlogger gets trapped during an avalanche, International Rescue must find a way to carefully dig him out(n)

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry investigate the case of a missing pineapple in Hawaii(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Toodles the Witch mesmerizes Tom in a haunted castle and poses a threat to Jerry and Tuffy(n)

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other(n)

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: As Fizz gets ready to travel to another dimension populated entirely by other Fizzes, Magoo turns up and thinks that Weasel has a new kitten.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean pimps his car in order to prove to Declan - a rival in love - that Bean's car is better.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Instructed to keep an eye on Gran while the rest of the Spuds visit to the doctor, Dave finds that she has escaped.(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po discovers that he has been poisoned and sets off to find who is behind it, while the students follow him in secret.(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a tour of Tobago, where he puts his own spin on some local recipes, including hand clapped rotis, dougla rum chicken, mustard fish and mango chili chow, and a bluewater breeze cocktail. He also helps Charlottesville fishermen to bring in the day's catch and takes a relaxing boat trip to the Nylon Pool(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up crispy party poppers with barbecue dipping sauce - ideal for movie nights - a breakfast burrito, aubergine curry and banana bread doughnuts. They are also joined by Prue Leith, who tucks into their mushroom wellington(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enjoys one of Britain's National Trails as she walks part of the Thames Path through Oxfordshire - meeting some of the people and animals who call the banks of this impressive river home. Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a special double elimination show, in which two more singers will be unmasked. Sharon and Kelly Osbourne are today's special guests on the panel, joining series regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall as they attempt to guess the identities of the six famous faces performing from behind bizarre costumes(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ann Widdecombe, Sam Nixon and Mark Rhodes and Jimmy Carr pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform fairy-tale routines, hoping to get a high enough score from the judges and viewers to keep them out of the bottom two. Sadly there'll be no happy ever after for Radzi Chinyanganya, who lost the skate-off last week, but while the votes are being counted there will be a routine from Disney on Ice. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. This time, the actor and his son arrive in Ohio, where they head for Dayton - the birthplace of aviation - before the pair attend a local rodeo later that evening. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: England v France. Jill Douglas presents action from both teams' opening match of the championship, which came from Stade de France. The hosts were looking to get off to a winning start under new head coach Fabien Galthie, while England were aiming to build on their run to last year's World Cup final. Plus, action from Wales v Italy at Principality Stadium and Ireland v Scotland at Aviva Stadium. With analysis from Jim Hamilton, Ugo Moyne, Gareth Thomas and Gordon D'Arcy, and commentary by Miles Harrison and Nick Mullins(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura travels to Edinburgh to try to uncover the truth behind Andrew's wife's death, but finds more than she bargained for. Meanwhile, tensions between Tom and Katy reach breaking point as their secret threatens to surface. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

On his return to the US following four tours of duty in Iraq, he finds living an ordinary life as a husband and father far more difficult to deal with than the demands of warfare. Biopic based on the experiences of real-life Iraq conflict veteran Chris Kyle, directed by Clint Eastwood and starring Bradley Cooper, Sienna Miller and Kyle Gallner(n)

programme.title: Rebound
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the quickfire quiz that tests the general knowledge of six contestants, who go head to head in a bid to walk away with a big cash prize. The players must give as many correct answers as they can in the time it takes the 'Rebound Bar' to move across the studio, with the money on offer rising accordingly(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean pimps his car in order to prove to Declan - a rival in love - that Bean's car is better.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Instructed to keep an eye on Gran while the rest of the Spuds visit to the doctor, Dave finds that she has escaped.(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po discovers that he has been poisoned and sets off to find who is behind it, while the students follow him in secret.(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a tour of Tobago, where he puts his own spin on some local recipes, including hand clapped rotis, dougla rum chicken, mustard fish and mango chili chow, and a bluewater breeze cocktail. He also helps Charlottesville fishermen to bring in the day's catch and takes a relaxing boat trip to the Nylon Pool(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up crispy party poppers with barbecue dipping sauce - ideal for movie nights - a breakfast burrito, aubergine curry and banana bread doughnuts. They are also joined by Prue Leith, who tucks into their mushroom wellington(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Known as the Paradise Islands, the Sporades' lush pine forests and coastlines are simply breathtaking. On Skiathos, Julia boards a fishing boat in search of a secret world of hidden coves and deserted beaches. Heading inland, she visits the hillside Monastery of Evangelistria, one of Greece's most important religious sites, before island-hopping to neighbouring Skopelos for a taste of marriage, Greek style. After trying on the island's traditional wedding dress, her trip ends at the tiny Chapel of St John(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enjoys one of Britain's National Trails as she walks part of the Thames Path through Oxfordshire - meeting some of the people and animals who call the banks of this impressive river home. Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a special double elimination show, in which two more singers will be unmasked. Sharon and Kelly Osbourne are today's special guests on the panel, joining series regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall as they attempt to guess the identities of the six famous faces performing from behind bizarre costumes(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ann Widdecombe, Sam Nixon and Mark Rhodes and Jimmy Carr pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform fairy-tale routines, hoping to get a high enough score from the judges and viewers to keep them out of the bottom two. Sadly there'll be no happy ever after for Radzi Chinyanganya, who lost the skate-off last week, but while the votes are being counted there will be a routine from Disney on Ice. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Chase presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together. This time, the actor and his son arrive in Ohio, where they head for Dayton - the birthplace of aviation - before the pair attend a local rodeo later that evening. Brad soon realises that they are not just there to spectate on the sport, they are also expected to take part. However, a nasty fall from an angry bull means Brad spends the rest of the night in hospital(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: England v France. Jill Douglas presents action from both teams' opening match of the championship, which came from Stade de France. The hosts were looking to get off to a winning start under new head coach Fabien Galthie, while England were aiming to build on their run to last year's World Cup final. Plus, action from Wales v Italy at Principality Stadium and Ireland v Scotland at Aviva Stadium. With analysis from Jim Hamilton, Ugo Moyne, Gareth Thomas and Gordon D'Arcy, and commentary by Miles Harrison and Nick Mullins(n)

As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Georgia Taylor (Coronation Street, Casualty) and Paterson Joseph (Peep Show) join the cast as the crime drama returns for its seventh series. In the first of a two-part story, a suicidal man causes a train crash when he drives onto a railway crossing, killing 15 people. With Jake prosecuting, the man's defence barrister tries to prove his vulnerability and mental illness, but then a twist in the investigation leaves Sam's career hanging in the balance. Starring Bradley Walsh, Dominic Rowan and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam is suspended from duty and decides to spend some time with his son, while Brooks and Leyton make a breakthrough when they discover who was responsible for the train crash and put the culprit under surveillance. Jake and Kate argue over whether it was right to prosecute Finn Tyler in his fragile mental state. Drama, starring Paul Nicholls and Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Forty-year-old mother of three Samantha Whyte turns to the programme for help as she searches for the Swiss father she has never met, having spent years dreaming about it. Raised by her single mother in Glasgow, Scotland, she was told the story of her parents' whirlwind romance in Zurich, and became desperate to know the man behind the myth. Meanwhile, Vanda James looks to put an end to a secret that has haunted her family for more than 50 years. When she was a child, a new-born baby was taken away at a few days old, and her mother never spoke about it. She discovered the baby was her brother and had been adopted, but her mother died before she had the courage to ask her about him(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Bert, a bouncy Great Dane puppy who is suffering growing pains, and needs surgery to prevent his front legs developing in an abnormal fashion. He also hears how caucasian shepherd dog Georgie is struggling to find a home due to her unusual appearance - her ears have been docked, giving her a scary look. Paul thinks a few website pictures might do the trick and calls on leading fashion photographer Rankin to help out. Finally, an injured Jack Russell is found by a council warden and rushed straight into surgery. Even if the operation is a success, can the dog hope to get over such a nasty experience?(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Labrador-lurcher Pluto, who has a rare condition that means he cannot swallow food properly. As a result he is not putting on any weight, leaving the Battersea vets scratching their heads wondering how to ensure he has a long and healthy life. Meanwhile, German spitz Hayes is scaring off prospective owners with his scary fang-filled smile, and when two-year-old cavachon Miley is dropped off at the home, she becomes terrified of her new surroundings. Can Paul cheer her up?(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: An African Journey
programme.desc: When the Duke and Duchess of Sussex took baby son Archie on their recent tour of southern Africa, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby was with them throughout. programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital

programme.desc: Bet is furious with Charlie for telling Tanya about Raquel's misfortune. Curly cooks Raquel a meal and tries to buck her up. Samir starts work at the Casablanca restaurant(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.desc: A vigilante group gets caught up in a shoot-out with a killer resulting in the death of an innocent bystander, and one of the group is charged with murder(n)

programme.title: Kojak

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur sells a less-than-roadworthy Austin Allegro to a nun - and it isn't long before a run of bad luck convinces him he's incurred the wrath of God. George Cole and Gary Webster play the entrepreneurial Daleys(n)

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: Darts: The Masters Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of the semi-finals and final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Michael van Gerwen continued his domination of this event in 2019, defeating Dave Chisnall in the last four and 2014 champion James Wade in the showpiece match to win the title for the fifth year in succession. However, Peter Wright's dominant victory over van Gerwen in January's World Championship final demonstrated the Dutchman is not invincible, and others will fancy their chances of taking this title. With analysis from Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little, and commentary by John Rawling, Stuart Pyke and Ned Boulting(n)

programme.title: Steven Gerrard: Make Us Dream (2018)
programme.desc: A look at the playing career of the former Liverpool captain, who progressed through the ranks at a time when the team were struggling for success and became a club legend. The documentary looks at his personal mission to restore the lift the Reds back to previous glories, and reveals how the supporters' love for him became a burden. This documentary captures the inside story of Farage's doomed attempt to persuade Boris Johnson to join a Leavers' Alliance with him, and also charts Farage's influence in changing British politics in the wake of the 2016 EU referendum. With racism returning to haunt the party and plummeting poll ratings, is December 2019's general election the event that finally persuades Farage that the UK will never buy what he is selling?
